questions.question: Explain the process of searching for a record in a static hash table using the division method. What is the best-case and worst-case time complexity?
keyPoints: The key is passed through h(k) = k mod m to compute the home address
The bucket at the computed index is checked for the record
Best case: O(1) when the record is found at the home address with no collision
Worst case: O(n) when all keys hash to the same bucket, requiring a full chain traversal

questions.question: Compare open hashing (separate chaining) and closed hashing (linear probing) as collision resolution strategies in static hashing. Discuss their advantages and disadvantages.
keyPoints: Open hashing: uses linked lists to chain overflow records, table never becomes truly full, extra memory for pointers
Closed hashing (linear probing): finds next empty slot within the table, no extra pointers but suffers from primary clustering
Chaining handles high load factors better; linear probing is more cache-friendly
Deletion is simpler in chaining; linear probing requires special deleted markers

questions.question: Given keys {18, 41, 22, 44, 59, 32, 31, 73} and hash function h(k) = k mod 7, insert all keys into a hash table of size 7 using (a) separate chaining and (b) linear probing.
keyPoints: Chaining: create linked lists at indices with multiple keys
Linear probing: resolve collisions by placing in next available slot
Show final state of hash table for both methods
